title: Document containers.conf settings for remote connections
body:
Currently we don't document which end of the podman-remote client server
operations uses the containers.conf.  This PR begins documenting this
and then testing to make sure the defaults follow the rules.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7657

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
@vrothberg We talked in a different issue about adding some tests to make sure that the server side is using containers.conf,  This PR begins that and fixes some issues, where we were relying on the client side and ignoring the server side.
On my review list for tomorrow. Brain isn't sharp enough to review so many line changes.

I just saw and reported [the same thing in 8170](https://storage.googleapis.com/cirrus-ci-6707778565701632-fcae48/artifacts/containers/podman/5933768118632448/html/int-remote-ubuntu-19-root-host.log.html). Pressing Re-run made it go away, which does not fill me with warm fuzzies.
